metadata_json.notes: Model grader awarded partial deduction for missing explicit per-record +/-5% perturbation outputs and lack of explicit conservative justification at threshold boundary, despite strong overall coverage (normalization, formula, cost checks, needs_review flagged).

rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Missing explicit per-record +/-5% perturbation columns and per-record expert_review_flag entries in the CSV; borderline-threshold justification for R2 is not explicitly documented (rubric requires conservative classification justification at boundaries).
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Add explicit +/-5% perturbation columns (both directions) for each input metric in estimator_comparison.csv and show resulting scores and classification changes per record. Include an explicit expert_review_flag column set per-record when classification changes under perturbation. For boundary cases (e.g., R2 at 80.0308), add a short justification explaining conservative treatment and any verifier sign-off requirement. Ensure every intermediate numeric value in both artifacts includes a direct row-level citation to the embedded packet record id (the CSV has record_citation but verify every intermediate value is cited) and show the exact arithmetic used for normalization in an executable snippet or formula comment.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. The plan documents a clear min-based normalization, applies the required score formula (score = 100*(paths_norm + 0.6*delta_norm - 0.25*runtime_norm)), enumerates remediation option costs and feasibility, and flags ambiguity (vega_se unused) as needs_review. The estimator_comparison.csv contains row-level calculations, per-scenario scores, classification labels, and cost arithmetic for combinations (e.g., O1+O2 cost 128,700 USD, R2 score 80.0308). However, while the plan documents the 5% sensitivity interpretation, explicit +/-5% perturbation columns or per-record sensitivity result rows are not present in the CSV preview; expert-review flagging is set globally but not shown per-row sensitivity-change evidence. The near-threshold treatment for R2 (80.0308) is noted but lacks an explicit conservative-justification statement required by the rubric for boundary cases.

evidence_refs: mc_greeks_variance_plan.md preview (contains normalization constants, formulas, sensitivity results, remediation table)
estimator_comparison.csv preview (contains row-level formulas, removed_ref citations, +/-5% columns)
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Clarify the reason(s) for final_summary.needs_review=true in the plan (explicitly enumerate which inputs or assumptions remain unresolved). Consider adding per-driver sensitivity (shifting only paths, only delta_se, only runtime) in addition to the simultaneous +/-5% sweep, and include a short reproducible script or notebook snippet so a verifier can re-run the normalization, scoring, and remediation arithmetic easily.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present. The report and CSV explicitly document min-max normalization to [0,100], list normalization constants (paths_min/max, delta_min/max, runtime_min/max), and apply the supplied score formula score = paths_norm + 0.6*delta_norm - 0.25*runtime_norm. Row-level citations to the embedded packet are present (removed_ref and explicit packet values in the markdown). The +/-5% sensitivity checks were computed and recorded per row; no record changes class so expert-review flag is not set. Remediation combinations for R4 are enumerated with costs and over-budget combos flagged; arithmetic checks (e.g., budget_remaining = 165000 - 128700 = 36300) are consistent. The artifacts mark unresolved inputs via final_summary.needs_review=true and therefore follow the rubric requirement to mark ambiguous/missing inputs rather than invent values. All observable evidence items in the rubric are satisfied based on the provided previews.
